stage1_tps_curve.framing_note: PRIMARY = composed-with-magically-free-head (the registered #561 +64.61-over-311.25 bar, the MOST generous to a fire). PHYSICAL (full-head, can't free the head) needs even more layers. Every bracket >> the quality cliff.

stage2_quality_curve.bake_separation_note: PURE int4 layer-drop (#538 raw-ablate, no bake/head-prune) 0.668->0.374 at 5L sits ABOVE osoi5's 0.274 collapse — the ~0.10 gap is the 12k head-prune (#547 -0.126) osoi5 also applied; the bake is ~neutral. puredrop_milder=True but it does NOT open a window: the pure drop still craters below 0.601 by ~2 layers.

stage3_verdict.verdict: depth-drop fire-region ANALYTICALLY EMPTY — #546 confirms, not discovers: clearing the +64.6 TPS bar needs >= 7 dropped layers even granting a magically-free head AND the largest per-layer saving (0.314 ms/layer); the int4 MMLU-Pro gate (0.601) breaks at <= 2 PURE-DROP layers (#538: 0.668->0.374 at 5L, layer-removal dominant); the quality cliff precedes the TPS bar by many layers, and every dropped block breaks #319 identity. The best quality-safe drop (~2 layers) yields ~263 TPS (full head), << the 375.857 ship.
stage3_verdict.two_ended_pin: ANALYTICAL end (this card): fire-region empty by construction. EMPIRICAL end (ubel #546, in flight): served depth Pareto. The sub-bar window (~2-layer drop -> ~263 TPS full head, << 375.857 ship) is the precise thing #546 must confirm.

#561.what: PARENT capstone: depth_drop_conjunction_plausible=False (asserted); registered bar gap_to_current_ship_tps=64.61 over 311.25 / 83.757 over 292.1. This card upgrades the assertion to a proof.

#554.what: TPS basis: bfh_tps 252.31 / tcycle 15.138 ms / E[T] 3.819; magically-free ceiling 311.25 (tcycle 12.271); osoi5 body-side served reduction 1.571 ms (5 layers) = direct-head-matmul-corrected -> per-layer ~0.314 ms; body weight-read 4.333 ms; 42-launch SDPA floor 0.573 ms.

#538.what: QUALITY basis: served body 2x2, greedy byte-identical. int4 42L 0.668 -> 37L 0.374; bf16 42L 0.656 -> 37L 0.448; layerdrop_cost_bf16 +0.208 (dominant) vs int4_cost_fullbody -0.012 (~free); dominant_body_knob=layer-removal; base_int4_clears_gate=True. PURE drop (raw-ablate, no bake/head-prune).

#548.what: the moat: shipped osoi5 (5-drop+12k-head+bake) MMLU-Pro 0.262 floored / 0.274 as-served; moat_is_genuine=True; first-token-EOS 0.0 (not an EOS artifact). Bake-separation reference.

#547.what: head/body split of the osoi5 collapse: head 31% / body 69%; 12k head-prune on INTACT body 0.676->0.550 (-0.126). Lets us subtract the head-prune from osoi5 to isolate the pure body-drop.

#539.what: offline drop-set Pareto: single-layer block-influence predictor rho=0.761; passers cluster late-sharpening L36-39; raw-ablate no-heal = NECESSARY-not-sufficient; contiguity hurts. Bake-damage / which-layers locus.

#543.what: reconciles #539: BI good as single-layer predictor, blind to composition (fails k>=5); drop=3 likely fails the offline screen. The most-generous quality-safe drop locus.

#546.what: the EMPIRICAL complement: served depth Pareto (TPS + quality at reduced depth), max_quality_safe_drop_count. This card pre-adjudicates it from the analytical end (two-ended pin).

#544.what: strict int4 precision ceiling 292.1; the 328.9 free-head pre-correction (#554 corrects to 311.25).

#515.what: >=90%-of-vanilla-base downstream quality gate; explicit MMLU-Pro floor 0.601 (= 0.90 * 0.668 anchor).
